title: Project: My Weekend Memories
objectives: Draw or paste pictures of weekend activities
Write descriptive sentences about what was drawn or pasted
Present weekend memories to the class (show and tell)

key_concepts.explanation: Students create a photo-strip style scrapbook of their last weekend. They draw or paste 4 pictures showing what they did, write sentences describing each picture, and present to the class. This practises past tense in a personal, meaningful context.

solution_steps: 1. Draw or paste a picture of what you did last weekend
2. Write sentences describing what you drew or pasted
3. Show and tell — present to class
examples.answer: A completed photo-strip with 4 drawings/pictures and descriptive sentences using past tense
examples.visual_description: A Polaroid camera with 4 photo frames in a strip, each with space to draw and lines to write

teaching_notes: This project practises past tense naturally — students describe what they actually did. The photo-strip format is fun and visual. Encourage use of: 'Last weekend, I went to...', 'I played...', 'We visited...'. The 'show and tell' component is good for speaking practice.
